title: Age-standardized rates + CIs + relative survival/stage + joinpoint trends (exemplar site)
context: With the canonical data cube (disparities-analysis-cube-008) in place and the methods standard (disparities-analysis-methods-002) approved, this task implements the core analysis module: age-standardized incidence and mortality rates with confidence intervals, relative survival and stage-at-diagnosis where available, and joinpoint trend analysis. All outputs use validated, citable epidemiology tooling (NCI Joinpoint Regression Program, Python lifelines/statsmodels or R epitools). Every output figure carries a Source; figures are regenerated from source so they cannot drift. Depends on: disparities-analysis-cube-008.
objective: Implement the analysis module (analysis/) to compute, for the exemplar cancer site: (1) age-standardized incidence and mortality rates using the 2000 US standard population, with Tiwari-modified confidence intervals and RSE/stability flags; (2) relative survival and stage-at-diagnosis distribution where available in open aggregate data; (3) joinpoint trend analysis (NCI Joinpoint Regression Program) with APC and AAPC for each stratum. All computations use validated epi tooling (NCI Joinpoint, Python pandas/numpy/lifelines/statsmodels, or R epitools). Every output figure is linked to its Source record and regenerated from source to prevent drift. The module is parameterized so a second cancer site is configuration, not a rewrite.
acceptanceCriteria: Computes age-standardized incidence and mortality rates using the 2000 US standard population for all race/ethnicity, sex, geography, and area-SES strata in the data cube
Attaches Tiwari-modified confidence intervals to all rates and RSE-based stability flags (unreliable at RSE > 30%); suppressed cells carry no computed rate
Computes relative survival and stage-at-diagnosis distribution where available in open aggregate SEER data for the exemplar site
Runs joinpoint trend analysis (NCI Joinpoint Regression Program) for each stratum with APC and AAPC reported; applies the minimum-points rule from disparities-analysis-methods-002
Uses validated, citable epi tooling only (NCI Joinpoint, Python pandas/numpy/statsmodels/lifelines, or R epitools); no bespoke reimplementation of age-standardization or joinpoint
Every output figure and table is linked to its Source record; figures are regenerated from pinned source data to prevent drift
Analysis module is parameterized so adding a second cancer site requires only configuration changes, not a code rewrite
Methods reviewer sign-off recorded confirming age-standardization, CI method, stability flagging, and trend modeling are correct (version-scoped)
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: A merged PR adding the age-standardized rates, CIs, stability flags, relative survival/stage, and joinpoint trend analysis module for the exemplar site, with every figure sourced and methods-reviewer sign-off recorded.
